name: Tryptophan sensor (TrpR + CRISPRi)
shortDescription: Detects tryptophan, a dietary amino acid and precursor of gut-microbial indole/serotonin metabolites. The classic TrpR repressor gates a CRISPRi circuit driving a fluorescent reporter, in the food-grade GRAS chassis Lactococcus lactis.

input.detects: Luminal/dietary tryptophan (precursor of indole and serotonin pathway metabolites)

sensing.sensorMechanism: TrpR binds tryptophan as co-repressor and represses the trp promoter.
sensing.signalFlow: Tryptophan -> Trp-TrpR represses Ptrp (so Ptrp drives the sgRNA only at LOW tryptophan); for a turn-on-with-tryptophan readout, route TrpR to gate a CRISPRi inverter. As drawn: tryptophan lowers sgRNA, de-repressing the reporter.

parts.name: TrpR repressor
parts.notes: Tryptophan co-repressor-activated; requires porting/codon-optimisation for L. lactis.

output.result: Signal reports tryptophan level (via the TrpR/CRISPRi inversion).

performance.notes: TrpR is one of the best-characterised repressors; integrated CRISPRi device is a design template. Choose inverter polarity to get a turn-on-with-tryptophan readout.

safety.biocontainment: thyA-based containment in L. lactis; food-grade host.
safety.notes: GRAS / food-grade chassis; research / supervised use.

buildSteps.title: Port + design
buildSteps.description: Codon-optimise TrpR/Ptrp for L. lactis; design the sgRNA and choose inverter polarity.

buildSteps.title: Assemble units
buildSteps.description: TU1: TrpR + Ptrp -> sgRNA. TU2: dCas9 + reporter. NICE vectors.

buildSteps.title: Expose to tryptophan
buildSteps.description: Validate across a tryptophan standard curve.

provenance.source: Design template combining the classic TrpR tryptophan repressor with L. lactis CRISPRi
provenance.license: Parts per their original sources; design template CC BY 4.0
